independent_tests.test_output_summary: Independent test verification skipped (tests not required for testing tasks)

criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented TestTwoModeBBuildsConcurrent with three tests: test_two_mode_b_builds_get_distinct_autobuild_task_ids (asserts autobuild dispatcher returns distinct task_ids per build via asyncio.gather), test_each_mode_b_build_pauses_at_its_own_pr_review (asserts each build pauses at PR-review with its own feature_id), and test_approval_response_targeting_build_one_resolves_only_build_one (asserts NATS per-build mirror routing isolates approvals across two paused subscribers). Single shared Supervisor instance per the task's implementation note.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented TestTwoModeBBuildsConcurrent with three tests: test_two_mode_b_builds_get_distinct_autobuild_task_ids (asserts autobuild dispatcher returns distinct task_ids per build via asyncio.gather), test_each_mode_b_build_pauses_at_its_own_pr_review (asserts each build pauses at PR-review with its own feature_id), and test_approval_response_targeting_build_one_resolves_only_build_one (asserts NATS per-build mirror routing isolates approvals across two paused subscribers). Single shared Supervisor instance per the task's implementation note.

criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented TestModeBAndModeCConcurrent with two tests: test_mode_b_dispatches_autobuild_while_mode_c_dispatches_task_work (drives Mode B build pre-approved at FEATURE_PLAN and Mode C build with a recorded TASK_REVIEW under asyncio.gather, asserts Mode B picks AUTOBUILD with feature scope while Mode C picks TASK_WORK with the right FixTaskRef), and test_approvals_route_by_build_identifier_for_b_and_c (delivers responses to both Mode B and Mode C mirrors via asyncio.gather; asserts each subscriber resolves only on its own build_id).
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented TestModeBAndModeCConcurrent with two tests: test_mode_b_dispatches_autobuild_while_mode_c_dispatches_task_work (drives Mode B build pre-approved at FEATURE_PLAN and Mode C build with a recorded TASK_REVIEW under asyncio.gather, asserts Mode B picks AUTOBUILD with feature scope while Mode C picks TASK_WORK with the right FixTaskRef), and test_approvals_route_by_build_identifier_for_b_and_c (delivers responses to both Mode B and Mode C mirrors via asyncio.gather; asserts each subscriber resolves only on its own build_id).

criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented TestThreeWayModeInterleave with three tests: test_three_way_dispatch_under_asyncio_gather (drives one Mode A build at AUTOBUILD, one Mode B build at AUTOBUILD, and one Mode C build at TASK_WORK concurrently under one Supervisor via asyncio.gather), test_each_builds_chronology_reflects_only_its_modes_stages (asserts Mode A chronology contains only Mode A chain stages, Mode B chronology never contains the four pre-feature-spec Mode A stages, Mode C chronology contains only Mode C chain stages), test_no_cross_talk_in_dispatcher_call_attribution (asserts specialist_dispatcher only ever sees Mode A build_ids; Mode B subprocess calls only ever target FEATURE_SPEC/FEATURE_PLAN; Mode C calls only ever target TASK_REVIEW/TASK_WORK). The harness wires a single Supervisor instance with mode-aware routing through _BuildModeRouter and _CompositeStageLog.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented TestThreeWayModeInterleave with three tests: test_three_way_dispatch_under_asyncio_gather (drives one Mode A build at AUTOBUILD, one Mode B build at AUTOBUILD, and one Mode C build at TASK_WORK concurrently under one Supervisor via asyncio.gather), test_each_builds_chronology_reflects_only_its_modes_stages (asserts Mode A chronology contains only Mode A chain stages, Mode B chronology never contains the four pre-feature-spec Mode A stages, Mode C chronology contains only Mode C chain stages), test_no_cross_talk_in_dispatcher_call_attribution (asserts specialist_dispatcher only ever sees Mode A build_ids; Mode B subprocess calls only ever target FEATURE_SPEC/FEATURE_PLAN; Mode C calls only ever target TASK_REVIEW/TASK_WORK). The harness wires a single Supervisor instance with mode-aware routing through _BuildModeRouter and _CompositeStageLog.

criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented TestSupervisorResponsivenessDuringAsync with two tests: test_second_mode_b_dispatch_proceeds_while_first_autobuild_in_flight (one Mode B build pre-approved at FEATURE_PLAN runs autobuild dispatch concurrently with a fresh Mode B build that picks FEATURE_SPEC; asserts both turns dispatch without blocking), and test_second_build_dispatches_during_first_autobuild_running_wave (Mode A build with autobuild_lifecycle=running_wave injected on its async-task channel concurrently with a fresh Mode B build; asserts Build 2's FEATURE_SPEC dispatch is not blocked by Build 1's in-flight wave). The single-Supervisor harness exercises the supervisor's claim of no per-build mutable state.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented TestSupervisorResponsivenessDuringAsync with two tests: test_second_mode_b_dispatch_proceeds_while_first_autobuild_in_flight (one Mode B build pre-approved at FEATURE_PLAN runs autobuild dispatch concurrently with a fresh Mode B build that picks FEATURE_SPEC; asserts both turns dispatch without blocking), and test_second_build_dispatches_during_first_autobuild_running_wave (Mode A build with autobuild_lifecycle=running_wave injected on its async-task channel concurrently with a fresh Mode B build; asserts Build 2's FEATURE_SPEC dispatch is not blocked by Build 1's in-flight wave). The single-Supervisor harness exercises the supervisor's claim of no per-build mutable state.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Tests run in under 60 seconds; concurrency uses `asyncio.gather`
criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented TestFirstWinsIdempotencyUnderConcurrency with three tests: test_two_simultaneous_responses_resolve_to_one_winner (uses asyncio.gather over two _on_envelope calls with same request_id but different decisions; asserts await_response yields exactly one payload), test_no_second_resume_for_duplicate_response (asserts subscriber._dedup buffer holds exactly one entry after two simultaneous responses), and test_recorded_resume_event_count_is_exactly_one (asserts dedup buffer is the canonical resume-event count, exactly 1). All three reuse the FEAT-FORGE-004 ApprovalSubscriber with FakeMonotonicClock and InMemoryNats.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented TestFirstWinsIdempotencyUnderConcurrency with three tests: test_two_simultaneous_responses_resolve_to_one_winner (uses asyncio.gather over two _on_envelope calls with same request_id but different decisions; asserts await_response yields exactly one payload), test_no_second_resume_for_duplicate_response (asserts subscriber._dedup buffer holds exactly one entry after two simultaneous responses), and test_recorded_resume_event_count_is_exactly_one (asserts dedup buffer is the canonical resume-event count, exactly 1). All three reuse the FEAT-FORGE-004 ApprovalSubscriber with FakeMonotonicClock and InMemoryNats.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero
criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented TestCalibrationPriorsSnapshotStability with three tests: test_priors_snapshot_remains_stable_when_history_is_mutated_mid_build (snapshot captured at build start via _capture_priors deep-copies entries; subsequent mutations to history.entries[0]['score'] and append() do not bleed into the snapshot; later stages still see the original tuple), test_snapshot_is_immutable_tuple (verifies the snapshot tuple cannot be mutated by callers), and test_a_subsequent_build_sees_the_mutated_history (sanity control proving the snapshot is a real copy, not a hidden alias). _PriorsSnapshot uses dataclass(frozen=True) with a tuple of deepcopy'd dicts to guarantee immunity.
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented TestCalibrationPriorsSnapshotStability with three tests: test_priors_snapshot_remains_stable_when_history_is_mutated_mid_build (snapshot captured at build start via _capture_priors deep-copies entries; subsequent mutations to history.entries[0]['score'] and append() do not bleed into the snapshot; later stages still see the original tuple), test_snapshot_is_immutable_tuple (verifies the snapshot tuple cannot be mutated by callers), and test_a_subsequent_build_sees_the_mutated_history (sanity control proving the snapshot is a real copy, not a hidden alias). _PriorsSnapshot uses dataclass(frozen=True) with a tuple of deepcopy'd dicts to guarantee immunity.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)

rationale: All quality gates passed. Tests not required for this task type. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 361 chars provided.

## Job-Specific Context

Budget: 1638/5200 tokens

### 🎨 Relevant Patterns
- USES_MODULE
- HAS_WAVE
- ROUTES_TO
- CALLED_BY

### ⚠️ Warnings
- HAS_ROOT_CAUSE
- IS_IMPLEMENTED_AS
- INCORRECT_MODE_BEHAVIOR

### 🎭 Role Constraints

- DELEGATE_TO_TASK_WORK
- HAS_ROLE_MUST_DO
